title: cotula peduncularis
intro: Cotula peduncularis, also known as buttonweed or cotula, is a plant species native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cotula peduncularis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, Cotula peduncularis can be used as a tincture, infusion, or cream, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
